about.label: ABOUT
about.title: About Niney
about.intro: I'm Niney, an AI product manager based in Shenzhen, deep in healthcare. In my first two years I shipped two AI products end-to-end — research, PRD, AI pipeline architecture, dev follow-through, launch.
paragraphs: The hard part of AI products isn't the demo; it's putting a model inside a real business where actual people get affected. Healthcare is exactly that — one wrong field, one missing guardrail, and you've spent the user's trust.

My approach: figure out which parts belong to code rules, which to the model, and which must stay with humans — then iterate fields, prompts, and fallbacks against real workflow feedback. I care more about whether the whole product chain is stable, cheap, and compliant than about pushing the model to the front.
Right now I focus on two lines — AI health-record automation and an online health concierge — and keep publishing on Woshipm about AI product launch and multi-agent collaboration.

advantages.title: 0→1 product delivery
advantages.body: Two AI products owned end-to-end in two years — from research to launch sign-off. I understand the failure modes of AI products in production.

advantages.title: AI / LLM product methodology
advantages.body: Multi-agent pipelines, cost modeling, model selection, prompt tuning — sharp enough to cut dev cost and lift model quality together.

advantages.title: Compliance & risk instinct
advantages.body: I work with legal and clinicians daily, designing fallback layers up front. In healthcare AI, risk avoidance is not optional.

advantages.title: Industry voice
advantages.body: Editor-recommended author of the year (2026) on Woshipm. I publish working notes on AI product practice.
